arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try various URL patterns for the debug symbol file
echo "=== Trying pattern 1: _.debug ==="
curl -sSI "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1 | head -5

echo "=== Trying pattern 2: libcoreclr.so.dbg ==="
curl -sSI "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg" 2>&1 | head -5

echo "=== Trying pattern 3: libcoreclr.so with sym ==="
curl -sSI "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" 2>&1 | head -5

echo "=== Trying pattern 4: libcoreclr.so without sym ==="
curl -sSI "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" 2>&1 | head -5
arguments.description: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The frames #01-#06 are from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. Frame #07 is JIT-compiled code (anonymous mapping).

To symbolicate these, I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` with that specific BuildId
2. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses


3. Microsoft publishes symbol files for .NET runtime binaries on their symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols`, where I can look up symbols using the BuildId to construct the proper path.

For ELF binaries on Linux, the URL pattern typically follows the format `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-<buildid>/libcoreclr.so` or a variant with the debug symbol extension. Let me try downloading the debug symbols using the BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801` from the tombstone. Let me try a few different approaches to get the symbols. I'll start by checking what tools are available on the system—like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line`—and also consider using the `dotnet-symbol` tool if it's installed, then attempt to download the symbols using whichever method works.
